fields.Text:
28-year-old woman with asymmetric pulses and concentric aortic wall enhancement. Most likely diagnosis?

{{c1::Takayasu arteritis}}
fields.Extra: Q1. Correct; difficulty: easy. Vital Concept: Takayasu arteritis shows concentric arterial wall inflammation most commonly affecting the subclavian artery, common carotid artery, and brachiocephalic trunk. MRI is the preferred first-line for imaging as it evaluates both vessel wall inflammation and luminal changes without radiation exposure.

fields.Text: Concentric arterial wall inflammation in a young woman with asymmetric pulses is characteristic of {{c1::Takayasu arteritis}}.
fields.Extra: Q1. Correct; difficulty: easy. Vital Concept: Takayasu arteritis shows concentric arterial wall inflammation most commonly affecting the subclavian artery, common carotid artery, and brachiocephalic trunk. MRI is the preferred first-line for imaging as it evaluates both vessel wall inflammation and luminal changes without radiation exposure.

fields.Text:
3-year-old with a pineal mass and spinal drop metastasis. Most likely diagnosis?

{{c1::Pineoblastoma}}

1/5
fields.Extra: Q2. Correct; difficulty: moderate. Vital Concept: The triad of very young age, pineal location, and CSF dissemination (evidenced by spinal enhancing lesion) is virtually diagnostic of pineoblastoma, which carries a poor prognosis despite aggressive treatment, making rapid diagnosis and staging crucial for treatment planning.

fields.Text: A pineal mass with CSF dissemination in a very young child is strongly characteristic of {{c1::pineoblastoma}}.
fields.Extra: Q2. Correct; difficulty: moderate. Vital Concept: The triad of very young age, pineal location, and CSF dissemination (evidenced by spinal enhancing lesion) is virtually diagnostic of pineoblastoma, which carries a poor prognosis despite aggressive treatment, making rapid diagnosis and staging crucial for treatment planning.

fields.Text:
Hyperthyroid patient with a diffusely hypervascular thyroid on Doppler. Most likely diagnosis?

{{c1::Graves disease}}
fields.Extra: Q3. Correct; difficulty: moderate. Vital Concept: Graves' disease creates "thyroid inferno" on Doppler with markedly increased parenchymal vascularity throughout the gland.

fields.Text: Marked diffuse thyroid hypervascularity on Doppler, or the thyroid inferno pattern, is characteristic of {{c1::Graves disease}}.
fields.Extra: Q3. Correct; difficulty: moderate. Vital Concept: Graves' disease creates "thyroid inferno" on Doppler with markedly increased parenchymal vascularity throughout the gland.

fields.Text:
CCK-HIDA scan demonstrates gallbladder emptying. What ejection fraction is the lower limit of normal?

{{c1::35%}}
fields.Extra: Q4. Correct; difficulty: moderate. Vital Concept: Values less than 35% are considered abnormal gallbladder ejection fractions.

fields.Text: On CCK-HIDA imaging, a gallbladder ejection fraction less than {{c1::35%}} is considered abnormal.
fields.Extra: Q4. Correct; difficulty: moderate. Vital Concept: Values less than 35% are considered abnormal gallbladder ejection fractions.

fields.Text:
Erectile dysfunction with calcified plaques along the tunica albuginea. Most likely diagnosis?

{{c1::Peyronie disease}}

1/2
fields.Extra: Q5. Correct; difficulty: easy. Vital Concept: Calcified plaques characteristic of Peyronie disease appear hyperechoic with acoustic shadowing; ultrasound has extremely high sensitivity for this entity and is an important tool for diagnosis and monitoring.

fields.Text: Calcified plaques of the penile tunica albuginea with erectile dysfunction are characteristic of {{c1::Peyronie disease}}.
fields.Extra: Q5. Correct; difficulty: easy. Vital Concept: Calcified plaques characteristic of Peyronie disease appear hyperechoic with acoustic shadowing; ultrasound has extremely high sensitivity for this entity and is an important tool for diagnosis and monitoring.

fields.Text:
Patient with recurrent nasal congestion and a cerebriform sinonasal mass. Most likely diagnosis?

{{c1::Inverted papilloma}}

1/3
fields.Extra: Q6. Incorrect; difficulty: hard. The CT demonstrates a mass located in the lateral nasal wall with focal coarse calcifications (yellow arrow), as well as bony resorption and extension into the maxillary sinus (green arrow).

fields.Text: A lateral nasal wall mass with a cerebriform enhancement pattern is characteristic of {{c1::inverted papilloma}}.
fields.Extra: Q6. Incorrect; difficulty: hard. The CT demonstrates a mass located in the lateral nasal wall with focal coarse calcifications (yellow arrow), as well as bony resorption and extension into the maxillary sinus (green arrow).

fields.Text: A cerebriform-enhancing lateral nasal wall mass is an {{c1::inverted papilloma}}, rather than allergic fungal sinusitis.
fields.Extra: Q6. Incorrect; difficulty: hard. The CT demonstrates a mass located in the lateral nasal wall with focal coarse calcifications (yellow arrow), as well as bony resorption and extension into the maxillary sinus (green arrow).

fields.Text:
Postmenopausal bleeding with the endometrial stripe shown. At what thickness is biopsy indicated?

{{c1::5 mm or greater}}
fields.Extra: Q7. Correct; difficulty: moderate. Vital Concept: In symptomatic postmenopausal women with abnormal uterine bleeding, an endometrial thickness ≤4mm on transvaginal ultrasound effectively excludes endometrial cancer with very high sensitivity, supporting current ACR and ACOG guidelines that recommend endometrial sampling for thickness ≥5mm.

fields.Text: In postmenopausal bleeding, endometrial sampling is recommended when endometrial thickness is {{c1::5 mm or greater}}.
fields.Extra: Q7. Correct; difficulty: moderate. Vital Concept: In symptomatic postmenopausal women with abnormal uterine bleeding, an endometrial thickness ≤4mm on transvaginal ultrasound effectively excludes endometrial cancer with very high sensitivity, supporting current ACR and ACOG guidelines that recommend endometrial sampling for thickness ≥5mm.

fields.Text:
Child with an obstructed duplicated collecting system and cystic lesion at the bladder insertion. Most likely diagnosis?

{{c1::Ureterocele}}

1/2
fields.Extra: Q8. Correct; difficulty: moderate. Vital Concept: In duplicated collecting systems, upper pole moieties typically have ectopic ureters with ureteroceles per Weigert-Meyer rule. Ultrasound shows asymmetrical renal length with dilated upper pole pelvicalyceal system due to obstruction. Ultrasound detects ureteroceles and hydronephrosis but may miss complete tract abnormalities.

fields.Text: In a duplicated collecting system, the upper pole moiety commonly obstructs because of an ectopic ureter with a {{c1::ureterocele}}.
fields.Extra: Q8. Correct; difficulty: moderate. Vital Concept: In duplicated collecting systems, upper pole moieties typically have ectopic ureters with ureteroceles per Weigert-Meyer rule. Ultrasound shows asymmetrical renal length with dilated upper pole pelvicalyceal system due to obstruction. Ultrasound detects ureteroceles and hydronephrosis but may miss complete tract abnormalities.

fields.Text: A thyroid uptake room is a {{c1::restricted area}} for radiation safety purposes.
fields.Extra: Q9. Correct; difficulty: easy. For regulatory purposes, areas in a typical nuclear medicine department are considered to be either restricted or unrestricted.

fields.Text: Achalasia results from loss of {{c1::inhibitory neurons of the esophagus}}, causing impaired lower esophageal sphincter relaxation and decreased peristalsis.
fields.Extra: Q10. Correct; difficulty: easy. Vital Concept: Achalasia results from a loss of inhibitory neurons in the esophagus, leading to delayed relaxation of the lower esophageal sphincter and reduced esophageal peristalsis. This may cause chronic dysphagia to solids and liquids. A barium esophagram and manometry are consistent with distal obstruction.

fields.Text: On mammographic triangulation, a mass that rises on the lateral view is located in the {{c1::medial breast}}.
fields.Extra: Q11. Correct; difficulty: moderate. Vital Concept: A useful mnemonic to remember this triangulation is muffins rise, lead falls. Medial masses rise on the lateral view and lateral masses fall on the lateral view.

fields.Text: Nephrogenic systemic fibrosis occurs primarily in patients with {{c1::acute kidney injury or severe chronic kidney disease}} after gadolinium exposure.
fields.Extra: Q12. Correct; difficulty: easy. Vital Concept: Nephrogenic systemic fibrosis is a potentially fatal fibrosing disease occurring in severe chronic kidney disease (estimated glomerular filtration rate less than 30) or acute kidney injury.

fields.Text:
Thoracic scoliosis with multiple plexiform neurofibromas. Underlying condition?

{{c1::Neurofibromatosis type 1}}
fields.Extra: Q13. Correct; difficulty: hard. Vital Concept: Neurofibromatosis type 1 occurs as a new mutation in 50% of cases, with the remaining 50% being familial (inherited in an autosomal dominant pattern).

fields.Text: Approximately half of cases of neurofibromatosis type 1 result from {{c1::a new mutation}} rather than an inherited mutation.
fields.Extra: Q13. Correct; difficulty: hard. Vital Concept: Neurofibromatosis type 1 occurs as a new mutation in 50% of cases, with the remaining 50% being familial (inherited in an autosomal dominant pattern).

fields.Text: Rewriting patient education material to improve comprehension exemplifies a commitment to {{c1::improving access to care}}.
fields.Extra: Q14. Correct; difficulty: easy. The radiologist’s attempt to make medical information more understandable for patients signifies improving access to care.

fields.Text:
Delayed enhancement of the inferior left ventricular wall. Which infarct territory is involved?

{{c1::Right coronary artery territory}}
fields.Extra: Q15. Correct; difficulty: moderate. The image shown is a delayed post-contrast short axis view of mainly the left ventricle.

fields.Text: Delayed enhancement involving the inferior myocardial wall is consistent with infarction in the {{c1::right coronary artery territory}}.
fields.Extra: Q15. Correct; difficulty: moderate. The image shown is a delayed post-contrast short axis view of mainly the left ventricle.

fields.Text:
30-year-old with hearing loss and a nonenhancing middle-ear mass medial to the ossicles. Most likely diagnosis?

{{c1::Acquired pars tensa cholesteatoma}}
fields.Extra: Q16. Incorrect; difficulty: hard. The CT image of the temporal bone shows a well-circumscribed, soft-tissue middle-ear mass located medial to the ossicles (red arrow).

fields.Text: A middle-ear cholesteatoma medial to the ossicles with lateral displacement of the ossicles arises from the {{c1::pars tensa}}.
fields.Extra: Q16. Incorrect; difficulty: hard. The CT image of the temporal bone shows a well-circumscribed, soft-tissue middle-ear mass located medial to the ossicles (red arrow).

fields.Text: A cholesteatoma medial to the ossicles with lateral ossicular displacement arises from the {{c1::pars tensa}}, not the pars flaccida.
fields.Extra: Q16. Incorrect; difficulty: hard. The CT image of the temporal bone shows a well-circumscribed, soft-tissue middle-ear mass located medial to the ossicles (red arrow).

fields.Text: When stereotactic biopsy specimen radiography shows calcifications but pathology does not, the next step is {{c1::radiography of the paraffin block specimen}}.
fields.Extra: Q17. Incorrect; difficulty: hard. Vital Concept: Imaging of the paraffin block specimen in this case helps to prevent a false-negative that may have resulted from undersampling of the specimen during pathological analysis. Ensuring concordance between prebiopsy imaging, specimen imaging and pathology helps to prevent false-negatives that can lead to delays in diagnosis.

fields.Text: If specimen radiography confirms targeted microcalcifications but pathology does not identify them, examine the {{c1::paraffin block radiographically}} before repeating biopsy or surgery.
fields.Extra: Q17. Incorrect; difficulty: hard. Vital Concept: Imaging of the paraffin block specimen in this case helps to prevent a false-negative that may have resulted from undersampling of the specimen during pathological analysis. Ensuring concordance between prebiopsy imaging, specimen imaging and pathology helps to prevent false-negatives that can lead to delays in diagnosis.

fields.Text:
Widened scapholunate interval with proximal capitate migration. Most likely diagnosis?

{{c1::Scapholunate advanced collapse (SLAC)}}
fields.Extra: Q18. Correct; difficulty: moderate. Vital Concept: Scapholunate advanced collapse (SLAC), and scapholunate ligament injury, can be seen in the setting of scaphoid fracture and/or nonunion. However, in this case, there is not a non-united fracture present or substantial post-traumatic deformity of the scaphoid.

fields.Text: Scapholunate dissociation with progressive capitate migration can lead to {{c1::scapholunate advanced collapse (SLAC)}}.
fields.Extra: Q18. Correct; difficulty: moderate. Vital Concept: Scapholunate advanced collapse (SLAC), and scapholunate ligament injury, can be seen in the setting of scaphoid fracture and/or nonunion. However, in this case, there is not a non-united fracture present or substantial post-traumatic deformity of the scaphoid.

fields.Text:
Young adult with upper-extremity swelling and subclavian vein thrombosis from thoracic outlet compression. Most likely diagnosis?

{{c1::Paget-Schroetter syndrome}}
fields.Extra: Q19. Incorrect; difficulty: moderate. Stent placement in the setting of Paget-Schroetter syndrome is not advised.

fields.Text: In Paget-Schroetter syndrome, {{c1::stent placement}} is generally avoided because repetitive extrinsic compression can cause stent fracture and restenosis.
fields.Extra: Q19. Incorrect; difficulty: moderate. Stent placement in the setting of Paget-Schroetter syndrome is not advised.

fields.Text: Paget-Schroetter syndrome may require thrombolysis, venoplasty, or rib resection, but {{c1::stent placement is generally not advised}} because of external compression.
fields.Extra: Q19. Incorrect; difficulty: moderate. Stent placement in the setting of Paget-Schroetter syndrome is not advised.

fields.Text:
Older adult with progressive dyspnea and basal subpleural honeycombing. Most likely diagnosis?

{{c1::Usual interstitial pneumonia}}
fields.Extra: Q20. Correct; difficulty: moderate. Vital Concept: Idiopathic pulmonary fibrosis is the most common interstitial pneumonia and is the term for the clinical syndrome associated with the histologic classification of usual interstitial pneumonia. Subpleural reticular opacities, macrocytic honeycombing, traction bronchiectasis, and the apicobasal gradient represent a constellation of signs that is highly suggestive of usual interstitial pneumonia.

fields.Text: Basilar subpleural reticulation, honeycombing, and traction bronchiectasis are characteristic of {{c1::usual interstitial pneumonia}}.
fields.Extra: Q20. Correct; difficulty: moderate. Vital Concept: Idiopathic pulmonary fibrosis is the most common interstitial pneumonia and is the term for the clinical syndrome associated with the histologic classification of usual interstitial pneumonia. Subpleural reticular opacities, macrocytic honeycombing, traction bronchiectasis, and the apicobasal gradient represent a constellation of signs that is highly suggestive of usual interstitial pneumonia.

fields.Text:
Acute testicular pain and fever with diffuse testicular hyperemia. Most likely diagnosis?

{{c1::Orchitis}}

1/2
fields.Extra: Q21. Correct; difficulty: easy. Vital Concept: Orchitis appears as enlarged, heterogeneous testicular parenchyma with invariably increased Doppler flow on color/power Doppler. Most cases occur with concurrent epididymitis, and demonstrating normal spermatic cord flow excludes testicular torsion—the critical differential diagnosis in acute scrotal pain.

fields.Text: An enlarged heterogeneous testicle with increased Doppler flow in acute pain and fever is characteristic of {{c1::orchitis}}.
fields.Extra: Q21. Correct; difficulty: easy. Vital Concept: Orchitis appears as enlarged, heterogeneous testicular parenchyma with invariably increased Doppler flow on color/power Doppler. Most cases occur with concurrent epididymitis, and demonstrating normal spermatic cord flow excludes testicular torsion—the critical differential diagnosis in acute scrotal pain.

fields.Text:
Severe cyclic pelvic pain with heterogeneous myometrium and small myometrial cysts. Most likely diagnosis?

{{c1::Adenomyosis}}
fields.Extra: Q22. Correct; difficulty: easy. Vital Concept: Adenomyosis diagnosis relies on junctional zone thickening with supporting findings of submucosal microcysts within the thickened junctional zone and ill-defined junctional zone borders on T2-weighted sequences.

fields.Text: Adenomyosis on MRI is characterized by a thickened T2-hypointense junctional zone with associated {{c1::T2-hyperintense myometrial cysts}}.
fields.Extra: Q22. Correct; difficulty: easy. Vital Concept: Adenomyosis diagnosis relies on junctional zone thickening with supporting findings of submucosal microcysts within the thickened junctional zone and ill-defined junctional zone borders on T2-weighted sequences.

fields.Text:
Hypotension after hepatic cryoablation with active arterial injury shown. Most likely diagnosis?

{{c1::Hepatic arterial hemorrhage with pseudoaneurysm}}
fields.Extra: Q23. Correct; difficulty: easy. This examination demonstrates the presence of a replaced right hepatic artery, arising from the SMA, instead of its normal location, arising from the celiac trunk.

fields.Text: Hepatic arterial hemorrhage after ablation can be managed with {{c1::endovascular coil embolization}}.
fields.Extra: Q23. Correct; difficulty: easy. This examination demonstrates the presence of a replaced right hepatic artery, arising from the SMA, instead of its normal location, arising from the celiac trunk.

fields.Text:
What retroperitoneal compartment is indicated?

{{c1::Perirenal space}}
fields.Extra: Q24. Correct; difficulty: moderate. That is the peri-renal space.

fields.Text: The retroperitoneal fat compartment surrounding the kidney is the {{c1::perirenal space}}.
fields.Extra: Q24. Correct; difficulty: moderate. That is the peri-renal space.

fields.Text:
What visceral arterial aneurysm is shown?

{{c1::Splenic artery aneurysm}}
fields.Extra: Q25. Incorrect; difficulty: not listed. Vital Concept: True splenic artery aneurysms greater than 3 cm require treatment due to rupture risk, while smaller asymptomatic aneurysms can be observed. Pseudoaneurysms and aneurysms in women of childbearing age require treatment regardless of size.

fields.Text: An asymptomatic true splenic artery aneurysm generally requires treatment once it is larger than {{c1::3 cm}}.
fields.Extra: Q25. Incorrect; difficulty: not listed. Vital Concept: True splenic artery aneurysms greater than 3 cm require treatment due to rupture risk, while smaller asymptomatic aneurysms can be observed. Pseudoaneurysms and aneurysms in women of childbearing age require treatment regardless of size.

fields.Text: The treatment threshold for an asymptomatic true splenic artery aneurysm is {{c1::greater than 3 cm}}, not 1 cm.
fields.Extra: Q25. Incorrect; difficulty: not listed. Vital Concept: True splenic artery aneurysms greater than 3 cm require treatment due to rupture risk, while smaller asymptomatic aneurysms can be observed. Pseudoaneurysms and aneurysms in women of childbearing age require treatment regardless of size.

fields.Text: In ultrasound, increased transmit power increases the risk of {{c1::thermal and mechanical bioeffects}}.
fields.Extra: Q26. Correct; difficulty: moderate. Vital Concept: Increased transmit power with ultrasound is associated with increased risk of bioeffects.

fields.Text:
Expansile skull-base lesion with multiple fluid-fluid levels. Most likely diagnosis?

{{c1::Aneurysmal bone cyst}}

1/2
fields.Extra: Q27. Correct; difficulty: not listed. Vital Concept: Aneurysmal bone cyst secondary to giant cell tumor appears as an expansile multiloculated T2 hyperintense lesion with characteristic fluid-fluid levels from altered hemodynamics causing intraosseous hemorrhage and blood-filled septated spaces.

fields.Text: An expansile multiloculated osseous lesion with fluid-fluid levels is characteristic of an {{c1::aneurysmal bone cyst}}.
fields.Extra: Q27. Correct; difficulty: not listed. Vital Concept: Aneurysmal bone cyst secondary to giant cell tumor appears as an expansile multiloculated T2 hyperintense lesion with characteristic fluid-fluid levels from altered hemodynamics causing intraosseous hemorrhage and blood-filled septated spaces.

fields.Text:
Intracavitary upper-lobe mass with an air crescent in a prior tuberculosis cavity. Most likely diagnosis?

{{c1::Aspergilloma}}

1/2
fields.Extra: Q28. Correct; difficulty: moderate. Vital Concept: Aspergillomas are mass-like balls found in the lung that are composed of the fungus Aspergillus fumigatus. Patients are typically asymptomatic when this diagnosis is made.

fields.Text: A mobile fungus ball in a pre-existing pulmonary cavity with an air crescent is an {{c1::aspergilloma}}.
fields.Extra: Q28. Correct; difficulty: moderate. Vital Concept: Aspergillomas are mass-like balls found in the lung that are composed of the fungus Aspergillus fumigatus. Patients are typically asymptomatic when this diagnosis is made.

fields.Text:
What vascular malformation has the popcorn appearance and susceptibility blooming shown?

{{c1::Cavernous malformation}}

1/2
fields.Extra: Q29. Correct; difficulty: moderate. Vital Concept: Developmental venous anomalies (DVAs) coexist with cavernomas in 30% of cases, appearing as caput medusae radial vessels draining to a collector vein. DVAs must be preserved during cavernoma resection to prevent venous infarction.

fields.Text: Developmental venous anomalies coexist with {{c1::cavernous malformations}} in approximately 30% of cases.
fields.Extra: Q29. Correct; difficulty: moderate. Vital Concept: Developmental venous anomalies (DVAs) coexist with cavernomas in 30% of cases, appearing as caput medusae radial vessels draining to a collector vein. DVAs must be preserved during cavernoma resection to prevent venous infarction.

fields.Text:
14-month-old with a large heterogeneous hemorrhagic mass demonstrating restricted diffusion. Most likely diagnosis?

{{c1::Atypical teratoid-rhabdoid tumor (ATRT)}}

1/3
fields.Extra: Q30. Incorrect; difficulty: hard. Vital Concept: Atypical teratoid rhabdoid tumor presents in very young children with heterogeneous mixed solid-cystic appearance and frequent intratumoral hemorrhage. Cerebellopontine angle involvement is characteristic when infratentorial. Shows restricted diffusion on DWI. Can occur supratentorially or infratentorially with aggressive clinical course.

fields.Text: A large heterogeneous hemorrhagic mass with restricted diffusion in an infant is characteristic of {{c1::atypical teratoid-rhabdoid tumor (ATRT)}}.
fields.Extra: Q30. Incorrect; difficulty: hard. Vital Concept: Atypical teratoid rhabdoid tumor presents in very young children with heterogeneous mixed solid-cystic appearance and frequent intratumoral hemorrhage. Cerebellopontine angle involvement is characteristic when infratentorial. Shows restricted diffusion on DWI. Can occur supratentorially or infratentorially with aggressive clinical course.

fields.Text: In an infant, a heterogeneous hemorrhagic CNS mass with restricted diffusion is concerning for {{c1::ATRT}}, not adult-type glioblastoma.
fields.Extra: Q30. Incorrect; difficulty: hard. Vital Concept: Atypical teratoid rhabdoid tumor presents in very young children with heterogeneous mixed solid-cystic appearance and frequent intratumoral hemorrhage. Cerebellopontine angle involvement is characteristic when infratentorial. Shows restricted diffusion on DWI. Can occur supratentorially or infratentorially with aggressive clinical course.

fields.Text: Posting a patient-identifiable radiograph publicly violates the professional responsibility of {{c1::patient confidentiality}}.
fields.Extra: Q31. Correct; difficulty: easy. The radiologist’s public posting of an identifiable image is a violation of patient confidentiality.

fields.Text:
Older adult with painful enlarged bowed forearm bone. Most likely diagnosis?

{{c1::Paget disease}}
fields.Extra: Q32. Correct; difficulty: easy. Vital Concept: Paget’s disease of the bone is most common in older men, and presents with pain, increased bone size, bowing of the bones, kyphosis, and decreased range of motion. Osteolytic changes appear in the early phase, and sclerotic changes in later phases.

fields.Text: Enlarged, bowed bone with mixed lytic and sclerotic changes in an older adult suggests {{c1::Paget disease}}.
fields.Extra: Q32. Correct; difficulty: easy. Vital Concept: Paget’s disease of the bone is most common in older men, and presents with pain, increased bone size, bowing of the bones, kyphosis, and decreased range of motion. Osteolytic changes appear in the early phase, and sclerotic changes in later phases.

fields.Text:
Patient with tuberous sclerosis and a nonshadowing echogenic renal lesion. Most likely diagnosis?

{{c1::Angiomyolipoma}}
fields.Extra: Q33. Correct; difficulty: easy. Vital Concept: Non-shadowing echogenic renal lesion most likely represents angiomyolipoma. Absence of shadowing excludes calcification or stones.

fields.Text: A nonshadowing echogenic renal lesion in a patient with tuberous sclerosis most likely represents {{c1::angiomyolipoma}}.
fields.Extra: Q33. Correct; difficulty: easy. Vital Concept: Non-shadowing echogenic renal lesion most likely represents angiomyolipoma. Absence of shadowing excludes calcification or stones.

fields.Text:
What device is shown?

{{c1::Ionization chamber survey meter}}
fields.Extra: Q34. Correct; difficulty: moderate. Vital Concept: Survey meters are portable radiation detection instruments utilizing ionization chamber technology to measure ionization current proportional to radiation exposure, providing real-time dose rate measurements essential for area monitoring, contamination surveys, and ALARA compliance in nuclear medicine.

fields.Text: Portable radiation survey meters operate on the principle of an {{c1::ionization chamber}}.
fields.Extra: Q34. Correct; difficulty: moderate. Vital Concept: Survey meters are portable radiation detection instruments utilizing ionization chamber technology to measure ionization current proportional to radiation exposure, providing real-time dose rate measurements essential for area monitoring, contamination surveys, and ALARA compliance in nuclear medicine.

fields.Text:
What is the most likely diagnosis on this hysterosalpingogram?

{{c1::Salpingitis isthmica nodosa}}

1/2
fields.Extra: Q35. Correct; difficulty: moderate. Vital Concept: Salpingitis isthmica nodosa results in outpouchings of the fallopian tube that can be well depicted on hysterosalpingogram. Salpingitis isthmica nodosa is thought to be the result of chronic pelvic inflammatory disease and is associated with higher risk of ectopic pregnancy and infertility. Risk factors for chronic pelvic inflammatory disease include sexually transmitted infections, multiple sexual partners/unprotected intercourse, young age under 25, prior episodes of acute PID, and recent IUD insertion.

fields.Text: Multiple small fallopian tube outpouchings on hysterosalpingography represent {{c1::salpingitis isthmica nodosa}}, which is associated with chronic pelvic inflammatory disease.
fields.Extra: Q35. Correct; difficulty: moderate. Vital Concept: Salpingitis isthmica nodosa results in outpouchings of the fallopian tube that can be well depicted on hysterosalpingogram. Salpingitis isthmica nodosa is thought to be the result of chronic pelvic inflammatory disease and is associated with higher risk of ectopic pregnancy and infertility. Risk factors for chronic pelvic inflammatory disease include sexually transmitted infections, multiple sexual partners/unprotected intercourse, young age under 25, prior episodes of acute PID, and recent IUD insertion.

fields.Text:
Midline fat-containing mass adjacent to a dysgenetic corpus callosum. Most likely diagnosis?

{{c1::Pericallosal lipoma with callosal dysgenesis}}
fields.Extra: Q36. Correct; difficulty: moderate. The images depict a pericallosal lipoma with corpus callosal dysgenesis.

fields.Text: A fat-containing mass adjacent to the corpus callosum is a {{c1::pericallosal lipoma}}, which is associated with callosal dysgenesis.
fields.Extra: Q36. Correct; difficulty: moderate. The images depict a pericallosal lipoma with corpus callosal dysgenesis.

fields.Text:
What acute traumatic finding is shown on this radiograph?

{{c1::Displaced comminuted fractures of the radius and ulna}}
fields.Extra: Q37. Correct; difficulty: moderate. Vital Concept: In patients with displaced, comminuted fractures of the limbs, CT Angiogram is the optimal exam to evaluate for suspected associated vascular injury.

fields.Text: A displaced comminuted forearm fracture with an absent distal pulse should be evaluated with {{c1::CT angiography}}.
fields.Extra: Q37. Correct; difficulty: moderate. Vital Concept: In patients with displaced, comminuted fractures of the limbs, CT Angiogram is the optimal exam to evaluate for suspected associated vascular injury.

fields.Text:
Sudden knee pain with a subchondral medial femoral condyle fracture line and marrow edema. Most likely diagnosis?

{{c1::Subchondral insufficiency fracture}}

1/2
fields.Extra: Q38. Correct; difficulty: moderate. Vital Concept: An insufficiency fracture occurs when the mechanical strength of a bone is reduced to the point at which a stress that would not fracture a healthy bone breaks the weak one. The condition that causes reduced bone strength typically does so throughout the skeleton (eg, osteoporosis, osteomalacia, or osteogenesis imperfecta) but may be more localized (eg, demineralization in a limb due to disuse). Progression of insufficiency fracture can result in osteoarthritis.

fields.Text: A subchondral insufficiency fracture of the knee can progress to {{c1::osteoarthritis}}.
fields.Extra: Q38. Correct; difficulty: moderate. Vital Concept: An insufficiency fracture occurs when the mechanical strength of a bone is reduced to the point at which a stress that would not fracture a healthy bone breaks the weak one. The condition that causes reduced bone strength typically does so throughout the skeleton (eg, osteoporosis, osteomalacia, or osteogenesis imperfecta) but may be more localized (eg, demineralization in a limb due to disuse). Progression of insufficiency fracture can result in osteoarthritis.

fields.Text:
What retroperitoneal compartment is indicated?

{{c1::Anterior pararenal space}}
fields.Extra: Q39. Correct; difficulty: moderate. Points to the anterior para-renal space.

fields.Text: The retroperitoneal space located anterior to the perirenal fascia is the {{c1::anterior pararenal space}}.
fields.Extra: Q39. Correct; difficulty: moderate. Points to the anterior para-renal space.

fields.Text: High reliability organizations contain adverse events through commitment to resilience and {{c1::deference to expertise}}.
fields.Extra: Q40. Correct; difficulty: not listed. Vital Concept: High reliability organizations contain unexpected adverse events through

fields.Text:
Young nonsmoking woman with dyspnea, prior pneumothorax, and diffuse thin-walled lung cysts. Most likely diagnosis?

{{c1::Pulmonary lymphangioleiomyomatosis (LAM)}}
fields.Extra: Q41. Correct; difficulty: moderate. Vital Concept: Pulmonary lymphangioleiomyomatosis (LAM) causes multiple thin-walled cysts on CT imaging. The most common symptoms are progressive dyspnea and fatigue.

fields.Text: Diffuse thin-walled lung cysts in a young nonsmoking woman with prior spontaneous pneumothorax are characteristic of {{c1::pulmonary lymphangioleiomyomatosis (LAM)}}.
fields.Extra: Q41. Correct; difficulty: moderate. Vital Concept: Pulmonary lymphangioleiomyomatosis (LAM) causes multiple thin-walled cysts on CT imaging. The most common symptoms are progressive dyspnea and fatigue.

fields.Text: Replacing aluminum filtration with copper filtration lowers patient dose by removing {{c1::low-energy photons that would be absorbed without improving the image}}.
fields.Extra: Q42. Correct; difficulty: hard. Vital Concept: Copper's higher atomic number creates superior low-energy photon filtration compared to aluminum, removing "useless dose" radiation that would be absorbed by patient tissues without contributing to image formation, resulting in lower patient dose with maintained or improved image quality.

fields.Text:
Paramidline cystic neck mass involving the hyoid bone. Most likely diagnosis?

{{c1::Thyroglossal duct cyst}}

1/2
fields.Extra: Q43. Correct; difficulty: easy. Vital Concept: Paramedian location with hyoid bone involvement and simple cystic signal characteristics are pathognomonic for thyroglossal duct cyst.

fields.Text: A paramidline cystic neck mass involving the hyoid bone is characteristic of a {{c1::thyroglossal duct cyst}}.
fields.Extra: Q43. Correct; difficulty: easy. Vital Concept: Paramedian location with hyoid bone involvement and simple cystic signal characteristics are pathognomonic for thyroglossal duct cyst.

fields.Text:
Severe chest pain after vomiting with pneumomediastinum. Most likely diagnosis?

{{c1::Boerhaave syndrome}}

1/2
fields.Extra: Q44. Correct; difficulty: moderate. Vital Concept: Forceful vomiting can cause esophageal perforation leading to a pneumomediastinum (Boerhaave syndrome). If perforation is suspected, it should be ruled out via CT or fluoroscopy with water-soluble contrast.

fields.Text: Suspected Boerhaave syndrome should be evaluated with {{c1::fluoroscopy or CT using water-soluble contrast}}.
fields.Extra: Q44. Correct; difficulty: moderate. Vital Concept: Forceful vomiting can cause esophageal perforation leading to a pneumomediastinum (Boerhaave syndrome). If perforation is suspected, it should be ruled out via CT or fluoroscopy with water-soluble contrast.

fields.Text:
Curved right lower-lung vascular opacity with right lung hypoplasia. Most likely diagnosis?

{{c1::Scimitar syndrome}}
fields.Extra: Q45. Incorrect; difficulty: moderate. Vital Concept: Scimitar syndrome shows the "scimitar sign" - a curved vertical opacity in the right lung representing anomalous pulmonary venous drainage to IVC, plus right lung hypoplasia and rightward shift of the heart and trachea.

fields.Text: Scimitar syndrome is partial anomalous pulmonary venous return to the {{c1::inferior vena cava}} with right lung hypoplasia.
fields.Extra: Q45. Incorrect; difficulty: moderate. Vital Concept: Scimitar syndrome shows the "scimitar sign" - a curved vertical opacity in the right lung representing anomalous pulmonary venous drainage to IVC, plus right lung hypoplasia and rightward shift of the heart and trachea.

fields.Text: Right lung hypoplasia plus a curved anomalous vein draining toward the IVC is {{c1::scimitar syndrome}}, not isolated right pulmonary hypoplasia.
fields.Extra: Q45. Incorrect; difficulty: moderate. Vital Concept: Scimitar syndrome shows the "scimitar sign" - a curved vertical opacity in the right lung representing anomalous pulmonary venous drainage to IVC, plus right lung hypoplasia and rightward shift of the heart and trachea.

fields.Text:
What breast density pattern is shown on this screening mammogram?

{{c1::Extremely dense breast tissue}}
fields.Extra: Q46. Correct; difficulty: easy. Vital Concept: Adjunct ultrasound in extremely dense breasts improves cancer detection but generates high false-positive rates requiring many additional biopsies. It remains a valuable radiation-free supplemental screening tool for intermediate-risk patients where mammography sensitivity is significantly reduced.

fields.Text: Adjunct screening ultrasound may improve cancer detection in patients with {{c1::extremely dense breasts}}.
fields.Extra: Q46. Correct; difficulty: easy. Vital Concept: Adjunct ultrasound in extremely dense breasts improves cancer detection but generates high false-positive rates requiring many additional biopsies. It remains a valuable radiation-free supplemental screening tool for intermediate-risk patients where mammography sensitivity is significantly reduced.

fields.Text: The maximum permissible dose rate in an unrestricted public nuclear medicine area is {{c1::2 mrem/hr (0.02 mSv/hr)}}.
fields.Extra: Q47. Incorrect; difficulty: hard. Vital Concept: Unrestricted public areas including waiting rooms must maintain dose rates below 2 mrem/hr (0.02 mSv/hr) to protect non-occupationally exposed individuals from unnecessary radiation exposure.

fields.Text: The unrestricted public-area dose-rate limit is {{c1::2 mrem/hr (0.02 mSv/hr)}}, whereas 0.5 rem is a total visitor/caregiver contact limit rather than an hourly waiting-room limit.
fields.Extra: Q47. Incorrect; difficulty: hard. Vital Concept: Unrestricted public areas including waiting rooms must maintain dose rates below 2 mrem/hr (0.02 mSv/hr) to protect non-occupationally exposed individuals from unnecessary radiation exposure.

fields.Text:
What Doppler index is calculated using this arterial waveform?

{{c1::Resistive index}}
fields.Extra: Q48. Correct; difficulty: easy. Vital Concept: Resistive index equals peak systolic velocity minus end-diastolic velocity divided by peak systolic velocity: RI = (PSV - EDV)/PSV, measuring vascular resistance to flow.

fields.Text: Doppler resistive index is calculated as {{c1::(peak systolic velocity - end-diastolic velocity) / peak systolic velocity}}.
fields.Extra: Q48. Correct; difficulty: easy. Vital Concept: Resistive index equals peak systolic velocity minus end-diastolic velocity divided by peak systolic velocity: RI = (PSV - EDV)/PSV, measuring vascular resistance to flow.

fields.Text:
What procedure is shown in this patient with hepatocellular carcinoma?

{{c1::Transarterial chemoembolization (TACE)}}
fields.Extra: Q49. Incorrect; difficulty: hard. Vital Concept: Primary risk factors for hepatic abscess post-TACE include bilioenteric anastomosis and incompetent sphincter of Oddi from stenting or sphincterotomy. These anatomic alterations enable biliary colonization by enteric flora, predisposing to ascending infection.

fields.Text: The greatest risk factors for hepatic abscess after TACE include bilioenteric anastomosis and an incompetent sphincter of Oddi from stenting or {{c1::sphincterotomy}}.
fields.Extra: Q49. Incorrect; difficulty: hard. Vital Concept: Primary risk factors for hepatic abscess post-TACE include bilioenteric anastomosis and incompetent sphincter of Oddi from stenting or sphincterotomy. These anatomic alterations enable biliary colonization by enteric flora, predisposing to ascending infection.

fields.Text: After TACE, biliary colonization caused by prior {{c1::sphincterotomy or biliary-enteric communication}} is a major hepatic abscess risk factor, rather than atherosclerosis.
fields.Extra: Q49. Incorrect; difficulty: hard. Vital Concept: Primary risk factors for hepatic abscess post-TACE include bilioenteric anastomosis and incompetent sphincter of Oddi from stenting or sphincterotomy. These anatomic alterations enable biliary colonization by enteric flora, predisposing to ascending infection.

fields.Text:
What thumb UCL injury is indicated by the arrows?

{{c1::Stener lesion}}
fields.Extra: Q50. Correct; difficulty: not listed. Vital Concept: A Stener lesion is a complete tear of the ulnar collateral ligament (UCL) from the thumb proximal phalanx at the level of the metacarpophalangeal (MCP) joint that is displaced superficial to the adductor pollicis aponeurosis, leading to interposition of the aponeurosis between the UCL and the MCP joint. The interposition of the adductor aponeurosis distinguishes the Stener lesion from other UCL injuries and impedes healing, thereby necessitating surgery.

fields.Text: A Stener lesion occurs when a torn thumb ulnar collateral ligament is displaced superficial to the {{c1::adductor pollicis aponeurosis}}.
fields.Extra: Q50. Correct; difficulty: not listed. Vital Concept: A Stener lesion is a complete tear of the ulnar collateral ligament (UCL) from the thumb proximal phalanx at the level of the metacarpophalangeal (MCP) joint that is displaced superficial to the adductor pollicis aponeurosis, leading to interposition of the aponeurosis between the UCL and the MCP joint. The interposition of the adductor aponeurosis distinguishes the Stener lesion from other UCL injuries and impedes healing, thereby necessitating surgery.
